stories.headline: US and Israel launch major strikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Khamenei and sparking widening regional conflict

articles.title: Iranian drone hit RAF base, Cyprus president says
articles.description: No casualties were reported and the base sustained "minimal damage", the Ministry of Defence said.

articles.title: Allies of US in the Gulf bear brunt of Iran attacks
articles.description: Iran's attacks on Gulf Arab states suggest the Islamic Republic is targeting not just the US military but also civilian infrastructure.

articles.title: Iran's regime is still intact - the coming days will show if it can hold out
articles.description: Iran's surviving leaders are in crisis mode, battling to project security as US and Israel strikes continue.

articles.title: UK will allow US to use bases to strike Iranian missile sites, PM says
articles.description: The prime minister says it remains the case that the UK will "not join offensive action now".

articles.title: Iran war widens, threatens to engulf Lebanon
articles.description: The war over Iran engulfed more of the Middle East and beyond on Monday as Lebanon's militant group Hezbollah responded to the killing of Iran's spiritual leader with its first attack on Israel in more than a year.

articles.title: Hezbollah strikes Israel as American and Israeli planes pound Iran
articles.description: Iran and Iranian-backed militias fired missiles at Israel and Arab states, apparently hitting the U.S. Embassy compound in Kuwait, while Israel and the United States pounded targets in Iran on Monday.

articles.title: Congress gears up for vote on Trump's war powers in Iran — after the battle began
articles.description: Democrats and a few Republicans are calling for curbing Trump's unilateral use of military power in Iran, despite previous such efforts failing to advance.

articles.title: 7 key points in U.S.-Iran relations since 1953
articles.description: The U.S. and Iran have a long history of tensions, including a CIA-led campaign to topple Iran's prime minister in 1953 and the taking of American hostages in 1979.

articles.title: Department of Homeland Security warns of potential attacks amid Iran operation
articles.description: The Department of Homeland Security has warned of potential lone-wolf and cyberattacks amid the ongoing strikes in Iran, according to a law enforcement bulletin.

articles.title: WATCH:  Protests, celebrations over US strikes on Iran
articles.description: In the U.S., Iranians and Iranian-Americans are taking to the streets to celebrate and protest the U.S. military strikes and the death of the Iranian supreme leader. ABC News' Jaclyn Lee reports.

articles.title: WATCH:  The latest as the US and Israel attack Iran
articles.description: The Middle East was rocked by another night of air attacks pitting the U.S. and Israel against Iran. ABC News' Chief Foreign Correspondent Ian Pannell reports from Tel Aviv, Israel.

articles.title: WATCH:  Americans scramble to leave Middle East amid strikes
articles.description: The fighting in the region is leaving thousands of Americans there uncertain about their situation. ABC News' Chief International Correspondent James Longman reports from Turkey.

articles.title: WATCH:  What strikes on Iran mean for the Middle East
articles.description: Former U.S. ambassador to NATO retired Lt. Gen. Doug Lute discusses the possible fallout from the joint U.S.-Israel attack.

articles.title: WATCH:  At least 201 people had been killed across Iran: Iranian Red Crescent Society
articles.description: Michael Singh, the managing director and senior fellow at the Washington Institute, joins to examine the U.S. military strikes in the Middle East.

articles.title: WATCH:  Missiles land in Dubai
articles.description: ABC News’ Lama Hasan reports from the region on the Iranian response to U.S. attacks, with the UAE saying a civilian was killed by falling debris.

articles.title: WATCH:  Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman says president, supreme leader 'safe and sound'
articles.description: Esmail Baghaei joins ABC News following US and Israeli strikes on Iran early Saturday morning

articles.title: Deaths, travel chaos mount as Iran lashes out at America's Gulf allies
articles.description: Day 3 of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran sees skies full of missiles as American allies take hits and deaths mount along with concern the conflict could spread.

articles.title: Trump says Iran operation could take "four weeks or less," 3 U.S. troops killed
articles.description: The U.S. military says three troops have been killed in the war with Iran, as President Trump says the operation is proceeding "ahead of schedule." Follow live updates.

articles.title: 3/1: Face The Nation
articles.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as the U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran continue into a second day after the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Senate Intelligence Committee Chairman Tom Cotton joins, as well as Sens. Chris Murphy and Ted Cruz.

articles.title: 3/1: CBS Weekend News
articles.description: Another wave of U.S. strikes on Iran; Iran launches strikes across Gulf countries.

articles.title: 3/1/2026: Iran; Under Siege; Breaking the Cycle
articles.description: First, Reza Pahlavi on the future of Iran. Then, a look at threats against U.S. judges under Trump. And, breaking the cycle of school shootings.

articles.title: World leaders and governments react to U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran
articles.description: U.S. allies and adversaries responded to the joint attacks by the U.S. and Israel on Iran with concern over the risks of a new war in the Middle East.

articles.title: Travelers stranded after Iran war closes airspace in the Middle East
articles.description: More than 2,400 flights were canceled Sunday across airports in the Middle East, according to flight tracker FlightAware.

articles.title: Americans stranded in the Middle East amid strikes
articles.description: Airstrikes have stranded thousands of Americans who were traveling to or through the Middle East. Shanelle Kaul reports.

articles.title: Trump says operations will "continue until all of our objectives are achieved"
articles.description: "Sadly, there will likely be more before it ends. That's the way it is. Likely be more," President Trump said after mentioning the three U.S. service members killed in the operation.

articles.title: As Trump Bombs Iran, America’s Allies Watch Fitfully From Sidelines
articles.description: Disregarded by President Trump over Iran, Europe’s leaders are adapting to a world in which they are little more than bystanders.

articles.title: Trump Says Iran War Could Last Weeks and Gives Competing Visions of New Regime
articles.description: In a brief interview, he said the country’s hardened military should simply surrender their weapons to the Iranian public.

articles.title: Trump Says More U.S. Casualties Are ‘Likely’ in War With Iran, and Oil Prices Jump After Attack
articles.description: Plus, what to know about the A.I. videos that kids are watching.

articles.title: Republicans Cite Iran Attacks to Pressure Democrats on Shutdown
articles.description: GOP lawmakers ensured there was enough money for the immigration crackdown, but the funding pause threatens other Homeland Security work.

articles.title: Trump says more American casualties ‘likely’ during Iran operation
articles.description: In a video post on Truth Social, President Donald Trump said combat operations in Iran would continue at “full force.” NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez details Trump’s full remarks.

articles.title: How Trump decided to strike Iran
articles.description: A last chance to avert war with Iran played out Thursday in Geneva, where Trump's U.S. negotiators told Iran they must not take steps to build a nuclear bomb.

articles.title: Khamenei's death met with public mourning, quiet celebrations as regime's opponents wait for an opening
articles.description: For the first time in 37 years, the sun rose on a leaderless Tehran, its streets unusually hushed as Iranians awoke to the news that Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei had been killed in joint U.S.-Israeli airstrikes

articles.title: Photos: Thousands of Iranians turn out to mourn Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran
articles.description: For many Iranians, the day began with the news they had feared.

articles.title: Attack on Iran threatens to explode into wider Middle East conflict
articles.description: As the U.S., Israel and Iran trade strikes in the Middle East, the conflict is threatening to explode into a much wider regional conflict. Iran is retaliating against U.S. allies like Kuwait, Bahrain, the United Arab Emirates and Jordan. NBC News’ Keir Simmons reports from Dubai.

articles.title: Khamenei's death met with public mourning, quiet celebrations as regime's opponents wait for an opening
articles.description: For the first time in 37 years, the sun rose on a leaderless Tehran, its streets unusually hushed as Iranians awoke to the news that Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei had been killed in joint U.S.-Israeli airstrikes

articles.title: U.S., Israel and Iran trade new strikes as conflict escalates
articles.description: Three U.S. service members have been killed in action and five more have been seriously wounded in military operations against Iran, the U.S. military said Sunday. It comes as Israel launched a new wave of strikes in Tehran as it continued to hit targets in Iran a day after the country's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed. NBC News’ Richard Engel reports from Tel Aviv.

articles.title: Republicans praise U.S. strikes on Iran as Democrats question the administration's strategy
articles.description: Members of Congress split largely along party lines in their assessments of the U.S. and Israeli military strikes on Iran, with Republicans mostly backing the operation and Democrats urging the administration to seek congressional approval and questioning President Donald Trump's strategy.

articles.title: Victims of missile strike outside of Jerusalem were taking cover in bomb shelter
articles.description: At least nine people were killed and dozens were taken to the hospital after missiles hit the Israeli city of Beit Shemesh outside of Jerusalem. NBC News' Matt Bradley reports from the scene where victims were taking shelter in a bomb shelter when the missiles struck.

articles.title: Mix of joy, unease grips Iranian diaspora watching U.S.-Israeli strikes from afar
articles.description: Across the United States, Iranian Americans expressed frustration, hope, dread and — above all — concern for relatives still in Iran after the joint U.S.-Israeli attacks.

articles.title: Outside White House, hundreds protest attack on Iran, urge end to conflict
articles.description: The protest near the White House was among the demonstrations that erupted across the country after Israel and the United States launched strikes on Iran.

articles.title: Europeans Spark Jokes Over Timing of Iran Response Meeting
articles.description: "War with Iran? Can't right now, it's the weekend," one social media user wrote in a post viewed over 600,000 times.

articles.title: How Donald Trump Won His Ultimate FAFO Feud
articles.description: The U.S. president and Iranian Ayatollah Ali Khamenei turned a meme war into an actual war. Only one of them came out alive.

articles.title: Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam announces ban on Hezbollah’s military activities
articles.description: Nawaf Salam says all Hezbollah activities are 'illegal' after the group launched attacks on Israel.

articles.title: Trump’s no-war promise vs Iran strikes
articles.description: We compare and contrast Trump’s campaign anti-war rhetoric with his decision as president to launch strikes on Iran.

articles.title: Iran death toll reaches 555 as US, Israel escalate attacks
articles.description: Civilian deaths are growing as more attacks are reported in the capital and other parts of the country.

articles.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s assassination will likely backfire. Here is why
articles.description: Assassinations provide a short-term political boost but lead to long-term disaster.

articles.title: ‘Missile debris’ sparks fire at Saudi’s Aramco oil refinery
articles.description: Videos show smoke rising from a refinery operated by Saudi after a fire broke out caused by debris from a missile.

articles.title: ‘Not again’: Gaza rushes to stockpile amid Iran war, crossing closures
articles.description: Crossing closures following US-Israel war with Iran spark panic buying in Gaza, where residents fear food shortages.

articles.title: What we know on day three of US-Israeli attacks on Iran
articles.description: A day after Iranian supreme leader's killing was confirmed, attacks are reported in Dubai, Cyprus and across the region.

articles.title: British military base in Cyprus targeted in suspected drone attack
articles.description: The attack marks the first strike on a British military base in Cyprus since 1986, escalating regional tensions.

articles.title: Congress returns to address Trump’s Iran attacks
articles.description: Morning Report is The Hill’s a.m. newsletter. Subscribe here. In today’s issue: ▪ Congress grapples with Iran attacks ▪ New pressure in DHS shutdown fight ▪ Paxton, Talarico lead Texas races: poll ▪ Cuba reaching breaking point Lawmakers are wrestling with how to respond to President Trump’s massive attack on Iran that eliminated many of…

articles.title: GOP, Trump set for war powers confrontation with Democrats after Iran strikes
articles.description: Lawmakers in both parties are bracing for a showdown in Congress this week over President Trump’s authorities as commander-in-chief after weekend U.S. strikes on Iran that killed that country’s supreme leader and threaten to embroil the nation in war. The Senate this week will debate and vote on a bipartisan war powers resolution sponsored by…

articles.title: Just a quarter of Americans back Iran strikes: Poll
articles.description: As the U.S. engages in another war in the Middle East, this time against Iran, a new Reuters/Ipsos poll found that roughly one in four respondents support it. The survey, released Sunday, found that 27 percent of respondents approve of “Operation Epic Fury,” which the U.S. launched in tandem with Israel early Saturday morning. Meanwhile,…

articles.title: Dem rep: Trump not responding to questions on servicemembers killed is ‘pathetic’
articles.description: Democratic Rep. Pat Ryan (N.Y.), an Army veteran, accused President Trump on Sunday of not looking out for U.S. troops, as three American service members have so been far been killed during the operation against Iran.  Speaking to Kaitlan Collins on CNN, Ryan said Trump not answering questions from reporters at the White House on…

articles.title: Trump administration to give briefing on Iran to Congress on Tuesday
articles.description: Top Trump administration officials will brief both chambers of Congress on Tuesday regarding ongoing U.S. strikes on Iran, amid widespread Democratic opposition to the operation and broad Republican support. Assistant Secretary of State for Global Public Affairs Dylan Johnson told The Hill that Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth, CIA Director…

articles.title: Israel closes Gaza border crossings amid strikes on Iran
articles.description: The Israeli government closed all border crossings into the Gaza Strip on Saturday, as the U.S. and Israel launch strikes against Iran.  Israel’s Coordination of Government Activities in the Territories (COGAT) wrote on the social platform X that the closure of the crossings is part of “several necessary security adjustments” amid the joint attacks on…

articles.title: Live updates: Trump says there will ‘likely be more’ American deaths in Iran conflict; Democrats push for war powers vote
articles.description: President Trump is urging for regime change in Iran and warning against retaliation, with Israel launching another round of strikes in the Middle Eastern country Sunday. Overnight, the president threatened to strike Iran “with a force that has never been seen before” if Tehran retaliates “very hard” against the U.S. Here’s what you need to…

articles.title: This week on The Hill: Lawmakers grapple with Iran conflict
articles.description: Lawmakers are grappling with President Trump’s decision to launch strikes against Iran over the weekend, setting off a widening conflict that is set to dominate Washington’s attention this week. The joint strikes with Israel killed Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and the conflict has spread as Iran launched retaliatory strikes against Israel and eight…

articles.title: Pope Leo amid Iran strikes: Stability and peace not achieved with ‘mutual threats’ or ‘weapons’
articles.description: Pope Leo XIV on Sunday said peace cannot be achieved through “mutual threats” or “weapons” amid strikes from the U.S. and Israel on Iran. “I am following with deep concern what is happening in the Middle East and in Iran during this tumultuous time. Stability and peace are not achieved through mutual threats, nor through…

articles.title: Trump says there will ‘likely be more’ American deaths in Iran before conflict ends
articles.description: President Trump spoke about the ongoing U.S. operation against Iran on Sunday afternoon and acknowledged it is “likely” that more U.S. service members will die. Earlier Sunday, U.S. Central Command (Centcom) said that as of 9:30 a.m. EST, three American service members have been killed in action, while five were injured and “several” others sustained…

articles.title: Strikes at Iran could reshape primary election dynamics
articles.description: Democratic candidates face new litmus tests as AIPAC money and U.S. intervention dominate primary races.

articles.title: Many of Trump’s own voters didn’t want to attack Iran. Now he has to win them over.
articles.description: President Trump is navigating an evolving issue where support within his coalition — at least before the strikes — was real but not overwhelming.

articles.title: Democrats split over response to Trump’s Iran strikes
articles.description: The breaks between the party’s peace and interventionist wings are ones Democrats will have to contend with ahead of the midterms.

articles.title: GORDON SONDLAND: No more 'restraint': Europe must stand with America on Iran
articles.description: Former U.S. ambassador to Europe criticizes Brussel's weak response to joint U.S.-Israeli strikes that eliminated Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and top officials.

articles.title: DOUG SCHOEN: As a Democrat, I back Trump's Iran strike — my party is wrong
articles.description: Democrat Doug Schoen defends Trump's Iran strike with Israel, criticizing his own party for condemning the action that killed Iran's supreme leader.

articles.title: Why Trump invoked regime change in attacking Iran, and the media must learn from past mistakes
articles.description: President Donald Trump spearheaded American participation in the strike that killed Ayatollah Khamenei – but the media should be cautious about rallying around the flag too hard.

articles.title: Tel Aviv analyst shelters from 30 missile sirens in 48 hours, says Iran won’t 'recover'
articles.description: Israeli security analyst calls the past 48 hours a "biblical event" as President Trump vows to continue military operations until objectives are achieved.

articles.title: Donald Trump says he took out Khamenei before ‘he got me’
articles.description: President Trump has said he took out Ayatollah Khamenei before “he got me” seemingly referring to the failed Iranian assassination attempts orchestrated against him. “I got him before he got me,” Trump told ABC News Sunday as he reacted to the death of the Iranian Supreme Leader, 86. “They tried twice. Well I got him...

articles.title: Hezbollah leader killed in IDF strikes in Lebanon
articles.description: The head of Hezbollah‘s parliamentary bloc in Lebanon’s government was killed during a military strike conducted by Israeli Defense Forces on Sunday. Mohammad Raad was among ten people killed by airstrikes in Beirut, Lebanon.  Raad was a member of the Lebanese Shia Islamist party and leader of the Loyalty to the Resistance Bloc, according to […]

articles.title: Israel struck Hezbollah targets across Lebanon, IDF says
articles.description: Israel launched targeted strikes on Hezbollah, an Iranian terrorist proxy, in Lebanon, its military announced on Sunday night. The Israeli military struck “senior” Hezbollah terrorists in Beirut and one “central” Hezbollah terrorist in southern Lebanon, according to its air force. It also urged civilians in nearly 50 villages in Lebanon to evacuate ahead of what […]

articles.title: Trump dodges questions on Iran after returning to Washington
articles.description: President Donald Trump did not speak to reporters after returning to Washington, D.C., on Sunday night, dodging questions about Iran and instead admiring new statues in the Rose Garden of the White House. The U.S. military began a large-scale military operation against Iran over the weekend with Israel, battering the regime’s military infrastructure and killing […]

articles.title: UK, France, and Germany jump into the fray in Iran, vowing to defend Gulf allies
articles.description: Three major European powers are setting up an increased defensive posture in order to defend their allies in the Gulf under assault from Iranian missiles. In a joint statement on Sunday, leaders of the United Kingdom, France, and Germany said they are “appalled” by Iran’s attacks on Gulf countries, which were launched in retaliation for […]

articles.description: Israel has emerged as the Middle East’s preeminent military power just as the U.S. seeks to reduce its permanent footprint in the region.

articles.description: The despot at the top of Iran’s clerical hierarchy since 1989 met his fate at the hands of the nations that he devoted his life to destroying.

articles.description: We watch the awesome display of American military might with humility, and wish Trump and the U.S. military every success.

analysis.summary: The United States and Israel launched joint military strikes on Iran beginning in late February 2026, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and triggering a widening regional conflict. Iran retaliated with missile strikes against Gulf Arab states, Israel, and other targets, while Hezbollah attacked Israel from Lebanon, three U.S. service members were killed, and Congress began debating war powers resolutions. The conflict disrupted air travel across the Middle East, prompted protests and celebrations worldwide, and drew international condemnation and concern about further escalation.
agreements: The US and Israel launched joint military strikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
Three US service members were killed and five were seriously wounded during the operation
Iran retaliated with missile strikes against Gulf Arab states including Kuwait, Bahrain, UAE, Qatar, and Jordan
Hezbollah launched attacks on Israel, and Israel struck back at targets in Lebanon
President Trump acknowledged more American casualties were 'likely' and said the operation would continue
Trump said the operation could take 'four weeks or less' and signaled openness to regime change
Congress split largely along party lines, with Republicans supporting the strikes and Democrats pushing for a war powers vote
Thousands of flights were canceled and Americans were stranded across the Middle East due to airspace closures
Israel closed Gaza border crossings amid the strikes
An Iranian drone struck a British RAF base in Cyprus, causing minimal damage and no casualties
UK, France, and Germany expressed concern and vowed to defend Gulf allies, though the UK said it would not join offensive action
Russia condemned the strikes as 'unprovoked armed aggression'
The Trump administration planned to brief Congress on Tuesday about the operation

disagreements.topic: Death toll in Iran

positions.position: Reports the death toll reached 555 in Iran, with growing civilian casualties
Reports at least 201 people killed across Iran, citing the Iranian Red Crescent Society
Reports deaths 'mounting' without specifying a total figure

disagreements.topic: Framing of the killing of Khamenei

positions.position: Calls it an 'assassination' and argues it will 'likely backfire,' leading to long-term disaster
Celebrates it as deserved fate for a 'despot' who devoted his life to destroying the US and Israel
Frames it as Trump winning his 'ultimate FAFO feud' against Khamenei
Highlights Trump's personal framing: 'I got him before he got me'

disagreements.topic: Whether the strikes were justified or legal

positions.position: Reports Pentagon told Congress there was no sign Iran was going to attack the US first
Features Alan Dershowitz arguing the strikes are constitutional and critics are 'dead wrong'
Reports Democrats and some Republicans are calling for curbing Trump's unilateral use of military power
Argues 'Iran chose the hard way' and regime change is the right course

disagreements.topic: Public support for the strikes

positions.position: Reports only 27% of Americans approve of 'Operation Epic Fury' according to Reuters/Ipsos poll
Reports many of Trump's own voters didn't want to attack Iran, and he now has to win them over
Features opinion pieces strongly backing the strikes, including from a Democrat (Doug Schoen)
Reports both protests and celebrations among Iranian Americans in the US

disagreements.topic: Emphasis on civilian harm vs. military objectives

positions.position: Leads with rising civilian death toll and humanitarian impact on Gaza from crossing closures
Emphasizes military success and strategic objectives, with Israeli analyst saying Iran won't 'recover'
Reports on deaths of Americans and Gulf allies while covering Trump's claim operations are 'ahead of schedule'
Focuses on Iran targeting civilian infrastructure in Gulf states and the regime's crisis mode

disagreements.topic: Europe's role and response

positions.position: Frames European leaders as sidelined bystanders 'disregarded by Trump,' adapting to irrelevance
Reports UK, France, and Germany 'jumping into the fray,' vowing to defend Gulf allies
Features criticism of Europe's 'weak response,' calling for them to stand with America
Highlights social media mockery of Europe's slow weekend scheduling of response meetings

disagreements.topic: Whether Khamenei was initially killed

positions.position: Aired Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman claiming the president and supreme leader were 'safe and sound' after initial strikes
Confirmed Iran's supreme leader was killed in the major US-Israeli attack
Reported Tehran woke up leaderless 'for the first time in 37 years' after Khamenei's death was confirmed

disagreements.topic: Trump's campaign promises vs. actions

positions.position: Directly contrasts Trump's 'no-war promise' campaign rhetoric with his decision to launch strikes
Reports Trump must now win over his own voters who didn't support attacking Iran
Frames Trump as decisively crushing a 'bloodthirsty regime,' with no mention of campaign anti-war rhetoric

stories.headline: Oil prices surge and global markets fall as Iran conflict threatens energy supplies

articles.title: Oil prices jump and shares fall as conflict escalates
articles.description: Experts have warned that a prolonged conflict could push global energy prices even higher.

articles.title: Oil prices soar and stock prices fall as US-Israel war with Iran rattles markets
articles.description: The U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran have sent a chill through world markets, with U.S. futures falling more than 1%

articles.title: Higher gas prices are likely coming to the pump after oil prices jump in wake of U.S. strikes in Iran
articles.description: The price of oil surged after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran, which killed its supreme leader.U.S.

articles.title: Oil prices rise sharply after US, Israeli attacks on Iran

articles.title: Gas prices: Costs creep up close to $3 per gallon to begin March
articles.description: The national average price for regular gas rose to $2.984 per gallon on Sunday to begin March.  The new pricing marks a five-cent increase from a week ago and slightly over an 11-cent rise since the beginning of February, when gas was $2.87 per gallon. With the current military conflict between Iran and the United […]

analysis.summary: U.S. and Israeli military strikes on Iran have triggered a surge in global oil prices and a sharp decline in stock markets, with supply disruptions across the Middle East raising fears of prolonged energy market turmoil. Brent crude jumped approximately 9%, U.S. futures fell more than 1%, and key energy infrastructure including a major Saudi refinery and the Strait of Hormuz shipping lane have been impacted. Analysts are warning of further price increases, with some projecting oil could reach $120 per barrel if the conflict is sustained.
agreements: Oil prices surged significantly following U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran
Global stock markets and U.S. futures declined in response to the escalating conflict
Energy supply disruptions in the Middle East are a central driver of market anxiety
Higher consumer gas prices are expected as a consequence of the oil price spike
The conflict involves coordinated U.S. and Israeli military action against Iran

disagreements.topic: Characterization and severity of the conflict

Uses the more restrained term 'conflict escalates'
Frames it as 'US, Israeli attacks on Iran' and notes Trump says strikes could last weeks
Reports the strikes killed Iran's supreme leader, suggesting a dramatic escalation

Cites Citi strategist saying Iran conflict is just one more worry added to an already stalled U.S. equity market in 2026

Talarico’s campaign blended prayer, pop music, and progressive politics at a rally in San Antonio on Sunday. […]

stories.headline: Supreme Court hears case on whether drug users can be barred from owning guns

articles.title: Supreme Court ponders law making it a crime for gun owners to use marijuana

analysis.summary: The Supreme Court heard oral arguments on whether a federal law barring drug users from possessing firearms is constitutional under the Second Amendment. The case involves a Texas man charged after drugs and a gun were found in his home, and the same statute was used to convict Hunter Biden. The case has created unusual political alliances, with both conservative gun rights groups and liberal civil liberties groups questioning the law's constitutionality.

The controversy has sparked broader debate about the ethics and regulation of betting on armed conflict.

stories.headline: North Korea's Kim Ju-ae increasingly visible, fueling speculation about succession

articles.title: Could Kim's teen daughter become North Korea's next leader?

articles.title: North Korea’s ‘most beloved’ child: what the key congress revealed about Kim Jong-un’s succession plans
articles.description:
Many observers believe North Korean leader has decided daughter Kim Ju-ae will succeed him, but others say gender politics could block her path to power
When North Korea’s ruling party held a top-level meeting this month there were predictable boasts of unstoppable nuclear development and, more unexpectedly, a suggestion by Kim Jong-un that his country and the US “could get along” – provided that Washington recognised North Korea as a legitimate nuclear power.
But for many North Korea watchers,

analysis.summary: Kim Jong-un's daughter Kim Ju-ae has become increasingly visible at official North Korean events, prompting widespread speculation among analysts about whether she is being groomed as the next leader. While many observers believe she is being positioned as successor, questions remain about her prospects given North Korea's patriarchal political culture.

Explicitly raises the issue of gender politics as a potential barrier to Kim Ju-ae's succession in North Korea's patriarchal system
Provides the specific context of a top-level party meeting this month featuring claims of nuclear development progress

stories.headline: Actor Awards ceremony held in Los Angeles with tributes and red carpet appearances

Prominent Hollywood figures attended the event

positions.position: Frames the event primarily as a visual, red carpet affair, highlighting celebrity appearances and photo opportunities
Frames the event around an emotional narrative — a posthumous award and tribute to Catherine O'Hara

stories.headline: US military mistakenly shoots down Customs and Border Protection drone on Mexico border

articles.title: Democrats outraged at US military’s downing of CBP drone near Mexico border
articles.description:
Second time in two weeks military used laser to attack what it mistakenly thought was a threat, disrupting air traffic

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Democratic members of Congress have expressed astonishment and anger at what they claim is the incompetence of the Trump administration after the US military used a laser on Thursday to shoot down what it thought was a threatening drone on the US-Mexico border in Texas but later turned out to belong

articles.title: WATCH:  FAA restricts airspace over El Paso after U.S. military shoots down border drone
articles.description: The U.S. military shot down a Customs and Border Protection drone it mistakenly believed was a danger using a high-powered laser.

analysis.summary: The US military mistakenly shot down a Customs and Border Protection drone near the US-Mexico border in Texas using a high-powered laser, believing it was a threat. The incident led to FAA airspace restrictions over El Paso and drew political criticism.
agreements: The US military shot down a Customs and Border Protection (CBP) drone
The military mistakenly believed the drone was a threat
A high-powered laser was used to shoot down the drone
The incident occurred near the US-Mexico border

disagreements.topic: Framing of the incident

positions.position: Frames the story primarily through the lens of Democratic political outrage and accusations of Trump administration incompetence
Frames the story primarily around the operational consequences, specifically the FAA airspace restrictions over El Paso

stories.headline: North Carolina Democratic primary puts Rep. Valerie Foushee's Israel stance in spotlight

articles.title: This House Democrat may lose her primary over past support for Israel
articles.description: The primary of Rep. Valerie Foushee, a North Carolina Democrat, is putting a spotlight on the shifting politics of Israel within the party.

articles.title: Progressives bet big on anti-Israel sentiment to oust Valerie Foushee in North Carolina
articles.description: An under-the-radar primary in North Carolina is gaining national attention after morphing into another competitive battleground for progressives waging war against establishment Democrats, putting incumbent Rep. Valerie Foushee (D-NC) at risk of losing her seat in the state’s bluest district. The 69-year-old Foushee is facing Durham County Commissioner Nida Allam, a younger, more left-leaning candidate, […]

analysis.summary: Rep. Valerie Foushee, a North Carolina Democrat, faces a primary challenge from Durham County Commissioner Nida Allam, with the race drawing national attention over Foushee's stance on Israel and broader progressive versus establishment dynamics within the Democratic Party.
agreements: Rep. Valerie Foushee is facing a primary challenge in North Carolina
The race involves the politics of Israel as a central issue
The challenger is Nida Allam, who is positioned to Foushee's left
The primary is attracting national attention

disagreements.topic: Framing of the Israel issue in the race

positions.position: Frames the race as reflecting 'shifting politics of Israel within the party,' suggesting a broader evolution in Democratic attitudes
Frames the race as 'progressives betting big on anti-Israel sentiment,' characterizing the challenger's position as anti-Israel rather than as part of a broader party shift

disagreements.topic: Framing of the broader political dynamic

positions.position: Centers the story specifically on Foushee's past support for Israel as the key vulnerability
Frames the race as part of a larger progressive war against establishment Democrats, with Israel as one component of a broader ideological battle

uniqueAngles.angle: Identifies Foushee's age (69) and describes the district as 'the state's bluest district,' providing specific demographic and political context
Identifies challenger Nida Allam specifically as a Durham County Commissioner
Characterizes the race as 'under-the-radar' before gaining national attention
Emphasizes that Foushee 'may lose' her primary, suggesting a stronger sense of electoral vulnerability

stories.headline: African countries push back against US bilateral health funding agreements under Trump

articles.title: Rising anger over ‘lop-sided’ and ‘immoral’ US health funding pacts with African countries
articles.description:
Zimbabwe refuses to sign agreement and Kenya faces a court case over data sharing as new aid deals come under scrutiny
A series of bilateral health agreements being negotiated between African countries and the administration of President Donald Trump have been labelled “clearly lop-sided” and “immoral” amid growing outrage at US demands, including countries being forced to share biological resources and data.
It emerged this week that Zimbabwe had halted negotiations with the US for $350m (£258m

articles.title: US accused of ‘shameless exploitation’ over proposed Zambian health aid deal
articles.description:
Leaked draft of $1bn memorandum of understanding reveals mandatory targets, sharing of data, and reported access to mining concessions
The US has been accused of “shameless exploitation” over a health financing agreement with Zambia worth more than $1bn (£740m), amid warnings that the country is getting a raw deal from the Trump administration.
A leaked draft of a five-year memorandum of understanding (MOU) between the two countries, seen by the Guardian, reveals that Zambia may accept terms wor
